name: Keyboard Shortcuts Demo
summary: The runnable proof of the keyboard-shortcuts block (#1139, #1041 audit §9): a chord-normalization module folds a raw KeyboardEvent into one canonical chord — folding the OS accelerator (Cmd on macOS, Ctrl elsewhere) onto a single Mod token — and a declarative shortcut registry fires the matching entry regardless of where focus sits, including across shadow boundaries. Graduates the block from draft to active.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof of the keyboard-shortcuts block (#1139, owner for the orphan block of #1041 audit §9). Two pieces are exercised live.

Chord-normalization module

Folds a raw KeyboardEvent into one canonical chord string (e.g. Mod+Shift+P). It reads .key/.code, folds the OS accelerator (metaKey on macOS, ctrlKey elsewhere) onto a single logical Mod token so the same author-written chord resolves on both platforms, and orders modifiers deterministically so two events with the same intent serialize identically.

Declarative shortcut registry

An author registers { chord, run } entries; one document-level dispatcher normalizes any incoming event and fires the matching entry. Because composedPath() lets the dispatcher see through shadow roots, a chord resolves regardless of where focus sits — including inside a shadow boundary.

In-demo implementation

The demo supplies its own in-demo implementation of both pieces — honest for a browser demo — to prove the block is realizable and graduate it from draft to active. The runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live (accelerator folding, deterministic modifier order, case-insensitive printable keys, named-key survival, author aliases, dispatch hit/miss, and focus-location independence); a live registry below it lets you try the chords on the real page.
